title: croton glandulatus
intro: Croton glandulatus, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy or Croton, is a plant species in the spurge family, characterized by its milky sap and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Croton glandulatus include pain relief, wound healing, and treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
